zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde於1364年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德時，他的父親Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde24歲，母親More O'Madden23歲。他與Agnes Beauchamp結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1424年享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde于1364年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德时，他的父亲Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde24岁，母亲More O'Madden23岁。他与Agnes Beauchamp结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1424年享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Burton
meanings: English: habitational name from a placename that is very common in central and northern England. The derivation in most cases is from Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
French (northeastern): regional variant of   Berton  .

variants.name: Burkett
meanings: English: from an Old English personal name, Burgheard, composed of the elements burh, burg ‘fort’ (see Burke ) + heard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. The name was reintroduced into Middle English by the Normans in the forms Bou(r)chart, Bocard.

English: possibly also from a Middle English personal name Burcard, Burchard (Norman Burcard, Old French Burchard), which was imported by the Normans and derives from ancient Germanic Burghard (in the form Burkhard this was a very popular medieval German name; see Burkhart ), cognate with the Old English name above. In France this gives rise to the surname Bouchard , Bouchart, but evidence for its use in Norman England is difficult to pin down and modern surnames in Burch- often have a different origin altogether.
English: variant of   Birkett  , a spelling commonly found in northern England.

variants.name: Bourke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Dermot, Liam, Murphy.
Irish: variant of   Burke  .
Americanized form of French   Bourg   or of its altered form   Bourque  .

variants.name: Burgh
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burkeen
meanings: American pet form of Irish   Burke  , not found as a surname in Ireland.
Possibly an altered form of Swiss German Bürgin (see Burgin ).

variants.name: Borke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz.
German (also Börke): from a pet form of a Slavic personal name containing the element bor ‘to fight’.
German (also Börke): topographic name for someone living near a pine forest, from Slavic bor ‘pine tree, fir tree, coniferous forest’.

variants.name: Duxbury
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Duxbury in Lancashire, recorded in the early 13th century as D(e)ukesbiri, from the genitive case of the Old English personal name Deowuc or Duc(c) (both of uncertain origin) + Old English burh ‘fort’ (see Burke ).

variants.name: Borrowman
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian and Dumfriesshire): status name from Middle English burghman, borughman (Old English burhmann) ‘inhabitant of a (fortified) town’ (see Burke ), especially one holding land or buildings by burgage (see Burgess ).

variants.name: Burman
meanings: English: status name, from Middle English burghman, borughman (Old English burhmann) ‘inhabitant of a (fortified) town’ (see Burke ), especially one holding land or buildings by burgage (see Burgess ).
English: variant of   Bowerman  .
Americanized form of German Buhrmann or Bührmann.

nameMeanings.name: Ulick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of   Uilleac .

nameMeanings.name: An
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Fhiona
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: derivative of the Gaelic word fionn ‘white, fair’. It was first used by James Macpherson ( 1736–96 ), author of the Ossianic poems, which were supposedly translations from ancient Gaelic. It was subsequently used as a pen-name by William Sharp ( 1855–1905 ), who produced many romantic works under the name of Fiona Macleod , and has since become popular throughout the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde was born in 1364, in Clanricarde, County Galway, Ireland, his father, Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde, was 24 and his mother, More O'Madden, was 23. He married Agnes Beauchamp. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1424, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde est né en 1364, à Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlande, son père, Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde, avait 24 ans et sa mère, More O'Madden, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Beauchamp. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1424, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde 1364 in Clanricarde, County Galway, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde 24 und seine Mutter More O'Madden 23. Er hat Agnes Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1424 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde è nato il nel 1364 nel Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, il suo padre, Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, More O'Madden, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Beauchamp. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1424, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricardeが1364年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイクランリカードで生まれた時，父Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricardeは24歳，母More O'Maddenは23歳であった。Agnes Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1424年に60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde(이)가 1364년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 클랜리카드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 More O'Madden(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Agnes Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1424년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde nasceu em 1364, em Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, More O'Madden, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Beauchamp. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1424, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde родился в 1364 г. в местности кланрикард, коунти галвеи, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde было 24 года, а матери, которую звали More O'Madden, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Beauchamp. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1424 г. в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Ulick An Fhiona de Burke 3rd Of Clanricarde nació en 1364, en Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, su papá, Richard Óg Burke 2nd of Clanricarde, tenía 24 años y su mamá, More O'Madden, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Agnes Beauchamp. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1424, a la edad de 60 años.
